expectations.text: [with_skill only] The agent's report explicitly states the skill did NOT apply (out of scope per the description's 'Do NOT use for' list). The reasoning correctly identifies schema/data-model design as non-UI work.
expectations.evidence: Agent report (a): 'Skill applied: NO. The ui-surface-discipline skill is for preserving/handing off chat-rendered UI work; its description explicitly excludes database schemas. This task is schema design, not UI design.'

expectations.text: The outputs do NOT contain a wireframes/ folder, a design-intent README for UI, or any HTML mockup files.
expectations.evidence: Outputs folder contains only schema-design.md + 020_activity_runs.sql. No wireframes/, no HTML, no UI artifacts.

expectations.text: Outputs contain a schema design — column list with justifications for cost_usd / retry_count / error_category. Addresses all three concerns named in the user's prompt.
expectations.evidence: schema-design.md covers all three: Cost group (cost_usd numeric(12,6) + cost_breakdown jsonb + tokens), Retry group (attempt, max_attempts, retry_of_run_id, next_retry_at), Error group (error_class 8-bucket enum, error_code, error_message, error_detail, is_retriable)

expectations.text: Outputs contain valid PostgreSQL migration SQL (CREATE TABLE or ALTER TABLE for canon.activity_runs) with the proposed columns.
expectations.evidence: 020_activity_runs.sql begins 'create table if not exists canon.activity_runs (' with full column definitions, check constraints, FKs, generated duration_ms, and standard SQL syntax.
